reasoning:
The question asks if there will be a deadly clash between Thailand and Cambodia, resulting in three or more fatalities, between August 1, 2025, and January 1, 2026. Very significant clashes occurred in July 2025 with dozens of casualties. Following this, tensions remained high. An attempted peace agreement brokered by Donald Trump fell apart in November after landmine incidents injured Thai soldiers. Most notably, a skirmish on November 12, 2025, left one Cambodian villager dead. 

However, this November incident did not meet the three-fatality threshold, and as of late November 2025, the community overwhelmingly predicted this question would resolve NO (8% implied probability). A targeted search for clashes or fatalities involving Thai and Cambodian troops in December 2025 yielded no evidence of recent major incidents. A broader search for recent clashes mostly turned up articles from the July conflict and the limited November incidents.

With less than a month remaining before the January 1, 2026 resolution cutoff, the odds of a major clash breaking out that meets the specified criteria are quite low, though slightly elevated due to the recent breakdown of the peace declaration. Given the lack of recent major escalations following the November breakdown, I estimate the probability of a 3+ fatality clash in the remaining 24 days to be roughly 4%.
